timelines.day0Definition: Date MAH/Sponsor first receives minimum information constituting valid case
timelines.keyDifferences: Aligned on 15-day timeline; validity criteria similar
timelines.notes: Clock starts on Day 0 (awareness date); submission by Day 15

timelines.day0Definition: Date MAH/Sponsor first receives minimum information
timelines.keyDifferences: FDA: Foreign serious unexpected to FAERS; EMA: All regions to EudraVigilance
timelines.notes: EMA requires global submission; FDA foreign only if serious+unexpected

timelines.day0Definition: Date sponsor first learns of event
timelines.keyDifferences: Aligned 7-day initial + 15-day total for fatal/life-threatening SUSARs
timelines.notes: Initial notification within 7 days; complete report within 15 days total

timelines.day0Definition: Date sponsor first learns of event
timelines.keyDifferences: Harmonized 15-day timeline for serious unexpected not fatal/life-threatening
timelines.notes: Submit to all regulatory authorities where trial ongoing

timelines.keyDifferences: FDA specific 3-day field alert; EMA uses urgent safety restriction process
timelines.notes: FDA: For bacteriological contamination, significant chemical/physical issues, or labeling mix-ups

timelines.keyDifferences: Major difference: EMA requires 90-day individual submission; FDA aggregates in periodic
timelines.notes: EMA: All non-serious to EudraVigilance within 90 days; FDA: In periodic reports only

timelines.keyDifferences: FDA still accepts PADER but prefers PBRER; EMA mandates PBRER format
timelines.notes: FDA: Within 30 days of end of reporting interval

timelines.keyDifferences: FDA accepts PBRER in lieu of PADER; EMA mandates PBRER per EURD
timelines.notes: Submit within 70 days (6-month/annual) or 90 days (3-year) after DLP

fda.timeframe: Annual, within 60 days of DIBD anniversary

timelines.keyDifferences: Harmonized annual DSUR; replaces IND annual report safety section
timelines.notes: DIBD = date of first authorization to conduct clinical trial anywhere globally

timelines.keyDifferences: FDA IND annual report predates DSUR; DSUR accepted as safety section
timelines.notes: Full IND annual report has broader scope than DSUR

fda.timeframe: Annual, within 60 days of approval anniversary

timelines.keyDifferences: FDA PMR/PMC tracking unique; EMA uses MA conditions and PASS
timelines.notes: FDA publishes annual PMR/PMC compliance status in Federal Register

fda.timeframe: 18 months, 3 years, 7 years minimum; more frequent if specified

timelines.keyDifferences: REMS-specific assessment; EMA uses RMP updates per variation schedule
timelines.notes: FDA may require more frequent assessments; eliminates REMS if goals met

ema.timeframe: With PSUR, post-referral, major change, or at EMA request

timelines.keyDifferences: RMP updated more dynamically than REMS; integrated with PSURs
timelines.notes: Submit updated RMP with Type II variation for significant changes

timelines.keyDifferences: VAERS unique to US; EMA uses EudraVigilance; WHO coordinates globally
timelines.notes: US HCPs also have mandatory VAERS reporting for Vaccine Injury Table events

timelines.keyDifferences: FDA-specific BPDR; EMA handles via GMP deviation reporting
timelines.notes: Report deviations affecting distributed products; eBPDR electronic system

timelines.eventReportType: Safety Labeling Change - Type II Variation

ema.timeframe: Type II variation; urgent safety restriction

timelines.keyDifferences: FDA CBE faster for urgent changes; EMA Type II variation standard
timelines.notes: FDA CBE-0 for immediate safety labeling; EMA urgent safety restriction for critical

ema.timeframe: Upon request; via PSUR signal summary

timelines.keyDifferences: EMA more structured signal communication; FDA via meetings and submissions
timelines.notes: MAH must respond to regulatory signal queries promptly

timelines.day0Definition: Date of determination of urgent safety issue
timelines.keyDifferences: Both allow immediate action; EU has specific 24-hour notification
timelines.notes: Used for serious and imminent risk to subjects/patients

ema.authority: Date MAH establishes safety issue meets emerging safety issue definition

timelines.ichReference: UK has most prescriptive timeline (3 working days vs EU "without undue delay")
Harmonized timeline; UK requires submission via MHRA Gateway
UK follows EU 90-day timeline; FDA periodic only

ema.timeframe: Per EURD list or MHRA request

timelines.ichReference: Category 1 products follow UK requirements; submit to MHRA

ema.authority: Data Lock Point per EURD list

timelines.ichReference: Category 2 generally not submitted to MHRA unless UK-only product

ema.timeframe: 15/90 days to MHRA Gateway AND EudraVigilance

timelines.ichReference: NI ICSRs require dual submission to both UK and EU systems; XI country code

fda.authority: N/A (EU uses Type II variation - requires approval)

ema.timeframe: N/A (UK follows EU Type II variation system)

timelines.ichReference: FDA CBE-0 allows immediate implementation of safety labeling strengthening without waiting for approval; EU/UK require pre-approval for equivalent changes
timelines.whoReference: FDA fastest; EU/UK slower due to approval requirement

timelines.ichReference: Certain labeling changes may be implemented 30 days after submission unless FDA notifies otherwise
When FDA issues safety labeling change notification, sponsor must respond within 30 days with proposed labeling or explanation
timelines.whoReference: FDA authority to require safety labeling changes

timelines.ichReference: Serious unexpected cases from literature reported same as spontaneous reports; Day 0 = date MAH becomes aware; Must include copy of article
Weekly screening enables timely identification of expedited cases; frequency should be justified based on product risk profile
timelines.whoReference: No prescriptive regulatory requirement but weekly is industry standard

timelines.ichReference: GCP requires immediate reporting of SAEs to sponsor; protocol typically specifies 24-hour timeline; exceptions may be protocol-defined

fda.timeframe: No statutory deadline; FDA responds within 30 days of complete submission

timelines.ichReference: Sponsor should respond promptly to clinical hold; FDA commits to 30-day response to complete submission
timelines.whoReference: US-specific clinical hold mechanism; EU/UK have equivalent urgent safety measure authorities

fda.timeframe: FDA: As specified in REMS; EU: Per GVP XVI Addendum I; UK: Per EU approach

ema.timeframe: EU PPP specifies testing schedule based on teratogenic risk

fda.timeframe: FDA: iPLEDGE monthly; EU: Per programme

ema.timeframe: Written acknowledgment of risks and contraception requirements

fda.timeframe: All regions: Verify effective contraception in place

ema.timeframe: Two effective methods required for highly teratogenic products
New from Reg 2024/1701; IAIN requires immediate notification

fda.timeframe: EU requires pre-approval; FDA CBE-0 allows immediate

ema.timeframe: Critical divergence: EU slower for safety labeling changes
All estimands with intercurrent event strategies documented
Pre-specify pooling strategies and consistency evaluation

fda.timeframe: Within 60 days of ANDA approval anniversary

timelines.keyDifferences: US-specific; EU generics have different PV obligations under decentralized/MRP
timelines.notes: Extends NDA PMR/PMC framework to generics; Public posting of compliance status

fda.timeframe: Within 60 days of BLA approval anniversary

ema.timeframe: Per MA conditions; RMP update schedule

timelines.keyDifferences: FDA annual report format; EMA uses PASS/RMP framework
timelines.notes: Biologics-specific; Immunogenicity studies often required

timelines.eventReportType: Quality Complaint with AE - PV Routing

ema.timeframe: Per GVP VI once AE identified
ema.authority: EU GMP Chapter 8; GVP Module VI

timelines.day0Definition: Date of AE awareness from complaint
timelines.keyDifferences: Quality/PV interface critical; Complaint may precede AE identification
timelines.notes: Manufacturing quality complaints must be screened for AE content

timelines.day0Definition: Date investigator becomes aware of SAE
timelines.keyDifferences: E6(R3) modernizes approach; Principles-based with fit-for-purpose documentation
timelines.notes: 2025 update; Addresses decentralized trials; Risk-proportionate monitoring

fda.timeframe: SAEs/AESIs per standard; Non-serious per protocol

timelines.keyDifferences: Late-stage trials only; Well-characterized safety profile required
timelines.notes: Reduces routine AE collection while maintaining signal detection

timelines.day0Definition: Date of awareness; Batch/donation link required
timelines.keyDifferences: Haemovigilance systems vary; Traceability critical
timelines.notes: Coordination between MAH PV and blood establishments required

timelines.keyDifferences: Format harmonized via ICH CTD; Regional Module 1 variations
timelines.notes: ISS format per M4E; Electronic submission via regional gateways

timelines.keyDifferences: UK-specific pathway; FDA Breakthrough/EMA PRIME are comparators
timelines.notes: Enhanced regulatory support; TDP integrates NICE/SMC

ema.authority: EU CMA: Reg 726/2004 Art 14-a; UK CMA guidance

timelines.keyDifferences: All regions require annual review of conditional approvals; Specific obligations tracked
timelines.notes: Failure to meet obligations may result in MA withdrawal

timelines.keyDifferences: EU provides most comprehensive MAH access via EVDAS; FDA more limited
timelines.notes: eRMRs support signal detection; Statistical outputs available

timelines.keyDifferences: PSMF is EU/UK-specific; US requires procedures but no master file
timelines.notes: Primary inspection document; Must reflect current system accurately

timelines.keyDifferences: Safety-driven adaptations per DMC; Pre-specification critical
timelines.notes: Includes stopping rules, sample size changes, arm dropping

timelines.keyDifferences: Core E2B(R3) harmonized; Regional IGs add specific rules
timelines.notes: Validation failures = rejected submission; Monitor acknowledgments

timelines.keyDifferences: Device timelines (30 days) differ from drug (15 days); Forms differ
timelines.notes: Combination products may require dual reporting

ema.timeframe: Immediate for serious public health threat

timelines.day0Definition: Date remedial action initiated or FDA request
timelines.keyDifferences: FDA 5-day expedited for remedial action; EU immediate for serious threats
timelines.notes: Triggered by remedial action need or FDA written request

fda.timeframe: Drug PMOA: 15 days; Device PMOA: 30 days

timelines.keyDifferences: PMOA determines primary timeline; May report to multiple centers
timelines.notes: Request for Designation (RFD) clarifies requirements

timelines.keyDifferences: Not MAH submission; National centres contribute
timelines.notes: WHO-UMC aggregates global data for signal detection

fda.timeframe: Continuous; Updates within 30 days of changes

timelines.keyDifferences: EU most advanced IDMP implementation; Global adoption evolving
timelines.notes: OMS, RMS, SMS, PMS maintenance requirements

timelines.keyDifferences: CIOMS VI provides practical guidance beyond ICH requirements
timelines.notes: Aggregate safety reviews; DMC interactions; Signal detection

fda.timeframe: EU: Per SPOR phases; Others: TBD

timelines.keyDifferences: EU leading implementation; Global timeline uncertain
timelines.notes: Foundation for global PV data interoperability

timelines.keyDifferences: FDA CBE-0 allows immediate implementation; EU Type II requires pre-approval (60-90 days)
timelines.notes: Parallel to 314.70 for drugs; Critical for rapid biologics labeling response

timelines.keyDifferences: US APR includes complaint review; EU PQR has similar scope
timelines.notes: Must include complaint trends - PV-relevant findings

timelines.keyDifferences: FDA registry-focused; EU PPP more prescriptive for teratogens
timelines.notes: Supports PLLR labeling updates

timelines.keyDifferences: FDA proactive; EU requires DHPC approval before distribution
timelines.notes: Must have process to monitor and respond to DSCs

timelines.keyDifferences: REMS assessment US-specific; EU uses RMP effectiveness evaluation
timelines.notes: May be required more frequently based on REMS

timelines.keyDifferences: Part 11 most prescriptive; EU Annex 11 principles-based
timelines.notes: PV databases must comply; Audit trail review critical

timelines.keyDifferences: OTC monograph system unique to US
timelines.notes: Different requirements from NDA products

timelines.keyDifferences: FDA active surveillance; May use to support PMR requirements
timelines.notes: MAH should understand Sentinel methodology

timelines.keyDifferences: Immunogenicity focus for all biosimilars; Interchangeability US-specific
timelines.notes: Enhanced PV for anti-drug antibody monitoring
